title: Sensex jumps 1,200 points in three days ahead of Iran war ceasefire expiry. 4 key factors
summary: Indian markets extended gains for a third straight session, supported by easing oil prices and optimism around US-Iran talks. Sensex and Nifty rose sharply, adding over Rs 8 lakh crore in market capitalisation. Broader markets and realty stocks led gains, while declining volatility signalled improving investor sentiment.

title: Trent shares jump 8% in two days as investors await bonus issue bonanza on April 22. What to expect?
summary: Trent shares surged after the company announced a board meeting to consider a bonus issue, dividend and fund-raising plans. The stock has gained sharply in recent sessions, supported by strong business momentum. Despite expected pressure on operating profit, analysts remain focused on the growth outlook driven by store expansion and steady revenue performance.

title: Lemon Tree Hotels shares soar 13%. What’s keeping traders active?
summary: Lemon Tree Hotels' shares surged over 13% following the announcement of its expansion into Gujarat with a new hotel signing. This move, part of a broader asset-light growth strategy, reinforces the company's focus on tourism and pilgrimage markets. The company is also undergoing a strategic restructuring with Warburg Pincus to unlock shareholder value.

title: Is the US stock market due for a deep correction amid stretched valuations and geopolitical risks?
summary: With the S&amp;P 500 nearing all-time highs, experts debate whether the US stock market is primed for a correction. Rising inflation, geopolitical tensions, and stretched valuations present risks.

title: Nestle Q4 Results 2026: Net profit rises 26% at  ₹1,114 crore; declares final dividend of  ₹5/share
summary: Nestle reported a 26% increase in Q4 net profit, reaching  ₹1,114 crore, up from  ₹885 crore last year. The company announced a final dividend of  ₹5 per share for the 2025-26 financial year.

title: Wipro share buyback: Should you participate or not in  ₹15,000-crore offer? Experts weigh in
summary: Wipro buyback: IT giant Wipro announced its biggest-ever share buyback on April 16, worth  ₹15,000 crore. Check out buyback details, along with expert opinions on whether you should participate or not.

title: Gold Price Outlook: Is the yellow metal still the most trusted asset for Indian households?
summary: Gold remains a key asset for Indian households despite an 8% price drop due to geopolitical tensions. The trend towards financial investments, such as ETFs, shows trust in gold is evolving rather than declining. Strong demand from central banks and domestic flows support a positive outlook for gold.

title: Sebi’s crackdown on options frenzy is bearing fruit, NSE data shows
summary: Small investor&nbsp; participation in the high-risk equity options segment dropped by nearly 20% in FY26 as tighter regulations and higher entry barriers began to rein in excessive speculation by retail investors.

title: Penny stock under Re 1 jumps over 8% after this acquisition update
summary: Penny stock under Re 1: NHC Foods share price jumped 33% in one month, and gained 12% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis. The penny stock has fallen 5% in six months, but gained 3% in one year.

title: Mehul Telecom IPO Day 3: Issue subscribed over 21x so far; NIIs steal the show
summary: Mehul Telecom IPO opened on April 17 and closes April 21, offering shares at  ₹96 to  ₹98. The company focuses on mobile devices and aims to raise  ₹28 crore, with a listing planned for April 24 on the BSE SME platform.

title: Sensex gains 1,300 points in 3 sessions: Can de-escalation in the US-Iran war drive it to 85K?
summary: The Indian stock market is buzzing with optimism as analysts predict a significant rise in the Sensex and Nifty 50, fueled by potential peace talks between the US and Iran. Can a final deal see Sensex soar to 85,000?

title: Stock market holiday: Why is the Israeli stock market closed today?
summary: Israel’s Memorial Day begins at sundown on April 20, marked by ceremonies, a nationwide siren, and restricted public activities. It will transition into Independence Day festivities, including fireworks and parades.

title: Infra.Market founders take on debt to pump more cash into firm as IPO plans slow
summary: Building materials platform Infra.Market’s founders are raising personal debt via a holding entity to inject equity into the firm, as IPO timelines stretch and valuation pressures mount.

title: Tax-exempt investment options in India — here are 5 EEE instruments for long-term savings
summary: Investors seeking post-tax returns can consider EEE instruments in India, which provide tax benefits throughout the investment cycle. While primarily found in government schemes, they offer a stable and safe option for traditional investors.&nbsp;

title: US Social Security: April's last payment to be sent tomorrow, are you on the list?
summary: The final April Social Security payments will be distributed this week, specifically on April 22 for beneficiaries born between the 21st and the month's end. The May payment cycle will start after that, with SSI payments expected on May 1, 2026.

title: 8th Pay Commission:  ₹69,000 minimum salary, 3.83 fitment factor; NC-JCM's 7 key demands explained
summary: 8th Pay Commission NC-JCM demands explained: They cover key issues such as salary, fitment factor, and pension reforms. The proposal includes a minimum salary of  ₹ 69,000. It also suggests a 3.833 fitment factor. The aim is to improve pensions and the overall pay structure.

title: Don’t apply for a visa before knowing this key cost difference
summary: Choosing between single-entry and multiple-entry visas depends on travel frequency, budget and plans. Single-entry visas are cheaper for one-time trips, while multiple-entry visas offer greater flexibility and convenience for frequent travellers, although they come with higher initial costs.

title: JPMorgan expands $1.5 trillion economic security splurge into Europe
summary: The banking giant is looking to invest in areas deemed critical to economic security, including defense and AI.
